label: Databricks
description: Query and manage Databricks SQL warehouses and lakehouses using a personal access token and HTTP path.

form.title: Connect Databricks
form.subtitle: Paste your workspace URL, personal access token, and SQL warehouse HTTP path to get started.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste your Databricks workspace URL, a personal access token (starts with dapi…), and the HTTP path of your SQL warehouse. Quickest way to connect — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Personal Access Token

Databricks personal access tokens (PATs) start with `dapi` and are the simplest way to authenticate programmatically.

### 1 — Find your workspace URL

1. Log in to your Databricks workspace.
2. Copy the URL from your browser address bar up to and including `.azuredatabricks.net`, `.gcp.databricks.com`, or `.databricks.com` — for example: `https://adb-1234567890123456.7.azuredatabricks.net`.

### 2 — Generate a personal access token

1. In your Databricks workspace, click your avatar (top-right) → **Settings**.
2. Navigate to **Developer → Access tokens → Manage**.
3. Click **Generate new token**, add a comment like `Anton`, and choose a lifetime (or leave blank for no expiry).
4. Copy the token — it starts with `dapi` and is shown **only once**.

### 3 — Find the SQL warehouse HTTP path

1. In the left sidebar, go to **SQL Warehouses** (or **Compute → SQL Warehouses**).
2. Click your warehouse → **Connection details** tab.
3. Copy the **HTTP Path** — it looks like `/sql/1.0/warehouses/abc123def456`.

### Notes

- Store the token securely — if lost, generate a new one and update this connector.
- Workspace admins can revoke tokens under **Admin Console → Access control → Personal access tokens**.
- If your workspace uses IP allowlisting, ensure Anton's host is permitted.

fields.name: workspace_url
fields.label: Workspace URL
fields.description: The base URL of your Databricks workspace, e.g. https://adb-xxxx.azuredatabricks.net or https://myorg.cloud.databricks.com.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Starts with dapi. Generated under Settings → Developer → Access tokens. Shown only once — store it safely.

fields.name: http_path
fields.label: SQL Warehouse HTTP Path
fields.description: Found in your SQL Warehouse → Connection details tab. Identifies which warehouse Anton queries.

fields.name: catalog
fields.label: Default Catalog
fields.description: Optional Unity Catalog catalog name to use by default. Leave blank to use the warehouse's default.

fields.name: schema
fields.label: Default Schema
fields.description: Optional default schema (database) within the catalog. Leave blank to use the warehouse's default.

methods.label: Service Principal (OAuth M2M)
methods.description: Use a Databricks-managed service principal with OAuth machine-to-machine credentials. Better for shared team environments or CI pipelines where a personal token is inappropriate.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Service Principal (OAuth M2M)

Databricks supports OAuth 2.0 machine-to-machine (M2M) flows via service principals. This avoids tying access to an individual user account.

### 1 — Create a service principal

1. In your Databricks workspace, go to **Settings → Identity & access → Service principals → Add service principal**.
2. Give it a name like `Anton` and click **Add**.
3. Note the **Application (client) ID** shown on the details page.

### 2 — Generate an OAuth secret

1. Open the service principal → **Secrets tab → Generate secret**.
2. Copy the **Secret** value — it is shown only once.

### 3 — Grant permissions

1. Assign the service principal to your SQL warehouse: **SQL Warehouses → your warehouse → Permissions → Add** the service principal with at least **Can use** privilege.
2. In Unity Catalog, grant the service principal the necessary catalog/schema/table privileges.

### 4 — Find your workspace URL and HTTP path

Same as the Personal Access Token method — see the SQL Warehouse → Connection details tab for the HTTP path.

### Notes

- The OAuth token endpoint is `https://<workspace-url>/oidc/v1/token`.
- Service principal secrets can be rotated without impacting other users.
- Workspace admins can manage service principals under **Admin Console → Service principals**.

fields.name: workspace_url
fields.label: Workspace URL
fields.description: The base URL of your Databricks workspace.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Service Principal Client ID
fields.description: The Application (client) ID of the Databricks service principal.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Service Principal Secret
fields.description: OAuth secret generated under the service principal's Secrets tab. Shown only once.

fields.name: http_path
fields.label: SQL Warehouse HTTP Path
fields.description: Found in your SQL Warehouse → Connection details tab.

fields.name: catalog
fields.label: Default Catalog
fields.description: Optional Unity Catalog catalog name. Leave blank to use the warehouse's default.

fields.name: schema
fields.label: Default Schema
fields.description: Optional default schema within the catalog. Leave blank to use the warehouse's default.
